zh.Tarrington, Herefordshire, England: 英國英格蘭賀福爾塔林頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Belet Lord Geyton於大約1170年出生在英國英格蘭多瑟特時，他的父親Robert Belet23歲，母親Lady Mabel Belet18歲。他和Lady Margaret De Marham至少有1個兒子。他去世於1215年11月享年46歲。

zh-hans.Tarrington, Herefordshire, England: 英国英格兰賀福爾塔林頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Belet Lord Geyton于大约1170年出生在英国英格兰多瑟特时，他的父亲Robert Belet23岁，母亲Lady Mabel Belet18岁。他和Lady Margaret De Marham至少有1个儿子。他去世于1215年11月享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: Belet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Bellew   2, a surname of probable French origin (see   Ballou   1 and 2).
English, Irish, and Scottish (of Norman origin): variant of   Bellew   1. This form of the surname has apparently died out in Britain.

variants.name: Bellew
meanings: English, Irish, and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from a lost or unidentified estate in or near Normandy named with Old French bel + eau ‘beautiful water’, as is clear from the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English surname spelling de Belewe and its Latin rendering as de Bella Aqua. The source of the surname is often stated to be Bellou (Orne), but this is improbable, as its etymological form Be(r)lou requires a different etymology than bella aqua. Nor is the surname from Belleau in Lincolnshire; this is post-medieval re-formation of the medieval placename Helgelo (Old Norse ‘Helgi's glade or meadow’).
Variant or Americanized form of   Ballou  , a surname of probable French origin. Compare   Ballew   1,   Balliew  ,   Belew   1,   Belue  , and   Billue  .
History: In Ireland this upper gentry surname (see 1 above) has been established since the 13th century in Duleek (Meath), where its estate has been known as Bellewstown since the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Belet Lord Geyton was born about 1170, in Dorset, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert Belet, was 23 and his mother, Lady Mabel Belet, was 18. He had at least 1 son with Lady Margaret De Marham. He died in November 1215, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Belet Lord Geyton est né environ 1170, à Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Belet, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Lady Mabel Belet, avait 18 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Lady Margaret De Marham. Il est décédé en novembre 1215, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Belet Lord Geyton um 1170 in Dorset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Belet 23 und seine Mutter Lady Mabel Belet 18. Er hatte mit Lady Margaret De Marham mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb im November 1215 im Alter von 46 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Belet Lord Geyton è nato il circa 1170 nel Dorset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Robert Belet, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Lady Mabel Belet, aveva 18 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Lady Margaret De Marham. Morì nel novembre 1215, all'età di 46 anni.
